name: KCC Farmers Market
tagline: Hawaii's biggest and best farmers market — 80+ vendors, incredible prepared food stalls, and a Diamond Head backdrop.
parking: Free parking at KCC campus; fills up fast
bestTime: Arrive at 7:30am Saturday opening; by 9:30am the best items sell out
talkStory: Founded in 2003 by the Hawaii Farm Bureau at Kapiolani Community College, KCC grew from a handful of tables into Hawaii's largest farmers market with 80+ vendors. Chef Ed Kenney and other local restaurant icons helped build its farm-to-table reputation by shopping here publicly. The Saturday market is a pilgrimage -- arrive at 7:30am opening or face the crowds. The Tuesday evening version is a local secret with the same vendors but a fraction of the Saturday mob.
